other_observers: Linda Litteral, Craig Dodson; Craig Dodson, 04/18/2013
specific_location: In Steamboat II subdivision, 4 miles west of Steamboat. Along street side near entrance of subdivision.
description: Male: finch sized with white cast to head and body. Distinctive black stripe through eye and bold black chest band. Tail had prominent white outer tail feathers. Chestnut patches on wings. Very long "spur" claw.  Female: finch sized, lighter brown striped body. Lesser chestnut patches on wings. Very long "spur" claw.Behaviors: Ground feeding much like horned larks. Flight was light with minor undulations. Gleaning seeds on roadside.Call: Flight call was a light pipingPlumage: Breeding
